action_set_definitions.name: get_forest_inventory
action_set_definitions.description: Retrieves the current state of the forest management environment, including financial data, ecosystem health, and detailed metrics for each forest plot.

action_set_definitions.name: execute_harvest
action_set_definitions.description: Harvests a specified percentage of timber volume from a plot. High intensity (e.g., >0.8) maximizes revenue but carries higher ecological costs.

plot_id.description: The unique identifier for the plot (e.g., 'P-101').

intensity.description: The fraction of total volume to harvest, between 0.0 and 1.0.

action_set_definitions.name: replant_trees
action_set_definitions.description: Initiates replanting of a harvested plot to ensure future sustainability and mitigate ecosystem health loss.

plot_id.description: The ID of the plot where replanting occurs.

species.description: The tree species to be planted.

action_set_definitions.name: apply_mitigation_strategy
action_set_definitions.description: Implements environmental conservation measures to improve the ecosystem health score.

action_set_definitions.name: step_simulation_year
action_set_definitions.description: Advances the simulation to the next year, allowing for natural timber growth and state updates.

example_output.message: Timber volume has increased due to natural growth.

state_description.current_year: The current year of the simulation cycle.
state_description.total_budget: Remaining financial resources available for harvesting and replanting operations.
state_description.forest_plots: A list of objects representing individual forest land units, containing id, tree_species, age, and estimated_volume.
state_description.ecosystem_health_score: A metric representing the biodiversity and environmental stability of the forest (0.0 to 1.0).
state_description.cumulative_revenue: Total revenue generated from timber sales since the start of the simulation.
